tree.setup: A busy intersection near a scenic area. Your phone's map app has led you in a circle. A woman selling roasted sweet potatoes from a cart, Auntie Luo, watches you walk past her for the third time. Her cart smells incredible. She's decided to intervene.
tree.setup_zh: 景区附近的十字路口。手机地图带你转了一圈。卖烤红薯的罗阿姨从推车后面看你第三次走过她面前。她的红薯闻起来太香了。她决定出手帮忙。

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 小伙子/姑娘！你是不是迷路了？你走了三圈了！
turns.text_pinyin: xiǎohuǒzi/gūniang! nǐ shì bu shì mí lù le? nǐ zǒu le sān quān le!
turns.text_en: Hey! Are you lost? You've walked past three times!

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Admit you're lost and ask for help.

options.text_zh: 是的，我在找西湖。手机地图不太好用。
options.text_pinyin: shì de, wǒ zài zhǎo xī hú. shǒujī dìtú bú tài hǎo yòng.
options.text_en: Yes, I'm looking for West Lake. The phone map isn't working well.
options.feedback: Auntie Luo laughs.「手机不如我！往那边走，过两个红绿灯就到了。」不太好用 means 'doesn't work well.' Local knowledge beats GPS every time.

options.text_zh: 不好意思。请问怎么去这个地方？
options.text_pinyin: bù hǎoyìsi. qǐngwèn zěnme qù zhè ge dìfang?
options.text_en: Sorry. How do I get to this place?
options.feedback: You show her your phone. Auntie Luo squints at the screen and points decisively.「哦，那个地方啊！从这里走十分钟。」怎么去 is the essential 'how to get to' question.

options.text_zh: 我没有迷路……好吧，我迷路了。
options.text_pinyin: wǒ méiyǒu mí lù... hǎo ba, wǒ mí lù le.
options.text_en: I'm not lost... OK, I'm lost.
options.feedback: Auntie Luo chuckles.「没关系！我在这里卖了十年红薯，每条路都认识。」Admitting defeat with 好吧 is honest and human.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 从这里一直走，到第二个红绿灯右拐，然后再走五分钟就到了。来，吃个红薯再走！
turns.text_pinyin: cóng zhèlǐ yìzhí zǒu, dào dì èr gè hónglǜdēng yòu guǎi, ránhòu zài zǒu wǔ fēnzhōng jiù dào le. lái, chī ge hóngshǔ zài zǒu!
turns.text_en: Go straight from here, turn right at the second traffic light, then walk five more minutes. Here, eat a sweet potato before you go!

options.text_en: Thank you! How much for a sweet potato?
options.feedback: 「五块！热的！」She picks the biggest one and wraps it in newspaper. The smell alone is worth the detour. Buying from someone who helped you is good karma.

options.text_zh: 太谢谢你了！第二个红绿灯，右拐，对吧？
options.text_pinyin: tài xièxie nǐ le! dì èr gè hónglǜdēng, yòu guǎi, duì ba?
options.text_en: Thank you so much! Second traffic light, turn right, correct?
options.feedback: Auntie Luo nods firmly.「对对对！走吧！」Repeating directions back shows you're paying attention. 对吧 asks for confirmation.

options.text_zh: 谢谢！我赶时间，下次吃红薯！
options.text_pinyin: xièxie! wǒ gǎn shíjiān, xià cì chī hóngshǔ!
options.text_en: Thanks! I'm in a hurry, sweet potato next time!
options.feedback: Auntie Luo waves you off.「别再绕圈了啊！」赶时间 means 'in a hurry.' She'll remember you if you come back.

tree.cultural_note: Roasted sweet potato carts (烤红薯, kǎo hóngshǔ) are a beloved street-side presence in Chinese cities, especially in autumn and winter. Street vendors often double as local guides -- they know every shortcut and landmark.
